sessions.name: IMPORTANT NOTE: BLOCK 1 STARTS WITH AN INTRO/DELOAD WEEK BEFORE INCREASING THE VOLUME/INTENSITY FOR THE REMAINING 4 WEEKS

exercises.name: IMPORTANT NOTE: BLOCK 1 STARTS WITH AN INTRO/DELOAD WEEK BEFORE INCREASING THE VOLUME/INTENSITY FOR THE REMAINING 4 WEEKS
Week 1
exercises.notes: Exercise Last Set Intensity Technique Warm-up Sets Working Sets Reps Tracking Load and Reps Early Set RPE Last Set RPE Rest Substitution Option 1 Substitution Option 2 Notes

exercises.name: Pull #1
exercises.notes: Wide-Grip Pull-Up N/A ~2-3 min Wide-Grip Machine Pulldown Wide-Grip Lat Pulldown 1.5x shoulder width overhand grip. Slow 2-3 second negative. Feel your lats pulling apart on the way down. Slight 0.5-1 second pause at the bottom, then lift your chest up and drive your elbows down as you lift yourself up. Don't be afraid to use assistance. Add weight if needed. Keep the form tight and controlled!

exercises.name: Push #1
exercises.notes: Meadows Incline DB Lateral Raise N/A ~1-2 min Cuffed Behind-The-Back Lateral Raise DB Lateral Raise Lie down on your side on a ~30° bench. Start with the DB in front of you, raise the DB to parallel to the floor and lower the DB behind you, feeling a deep stretch in your delts, then reverse this motion (2 reps have now been completed). No pausing between reps.

exercises.name: Legs #1
exercises.notes: Seated Leg Curl N/A ~1-2 min Lying Leg Curl Nordic Ham Curl Lean forward over the machine to get a maximum stretch in your hamstrings.

exercises.name:
Arms & 
Weak Points #1
exercises.notes: Weak Point Exercise 1 (optional) N/A ~1-3 min Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.

exercises.name: Pull #2
exercises.notes: Smith Machine Deficit Row N/A ~3-4 min Pendlay Deficit Row Helms Row Focus on getting a big stretch and touch your stomach/chest on each rep!

exercises.name: Push #2
exercises.notes: High-Cable Cuffed Lateral Raise N/A ~1-2 min High-Cable Lateral Raise DB Lateral Raise Focus on squeezing your lateral delt to move the weight.

exercises.name: Legs #2
exercises.notes: Barbell RDL N/A ~3-5 min DB RDL Deadlift The RPE is intentionally low here because these will cause a lot of muscle damage. Don't be tempted to go too heavy. To keep tension on the hamstrings, stop about 75% of the way to full lockout on each rep (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the range of motion).

exercises.name:
Arms & 
Weak Points #2
exercises.notes: Weak Point Exercise 1 (optional) N/A ~1-3 min Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.

exercises.name: Week 2
exercises.notes: Exercise Last-Set Intensity Technique Warm-up Sets Working Sets Reps Tracking Load and Reps Early Set RPE Last Set RPE Rest Substitution Option 1 Substitution Option 2 Notes

exercises.name: Pull #1
exercises.notes:
Wide-Grip Pull-Up Lengthened Partials 
(Extend Set) ~2-3 min Wide-Grip Machine Pulldown Wide-Grip Lat Pulldown 1.5x shoulder width overhand grip. Slow 2-3 second negative. Feel your lats pulling apart on the way down. Slight 0.5-1 second pause at the bottom, then lift your chest up and drive your elbows down as you lift yourself up. Don't be afraid to use assistance. Add weight if needed. Keep the form tight and controlled! Once you hit the Last Set RPE on the final set, switch to partial reps. These should be the bottom half of the ROM. Stop once you have 0-1 half reps left in the tank.

exercises.name: Push #1
exercises.notes: Meadows Incline DB Lateral Raise Failure ~1-2 min Cuffed Behind-The-Back Lateral Raise DB Lateral Raise Lie down on your side on a ~30° bench. Start with the DB in front of you, raise the DB to parallel to the floor and lower the DB behind you, feeling a deep stretch in your delts, then reverse this motion (2 reps have now been completed). No pausing between reps.

exercises.name: Legs #1
exercises.notes:
Seated Leg Curl Lengthened Partials 
(Extend Set) ~1-2 min Lying Leg Curl Nordic Ham Curl Lean forward over the machine to get a maximum stretch in your hamstrings. Once you hit failure on the final set, continue with lengthened partials in the top half of the ROM, until you can no longer achieve a full half rep.

exercises.name:
Arms & 
Weak Points #1
exercises.notes: Weak Point Exercise 1 (optional) N/A ~1-3 min Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.

exercises.name: Pull #2
exercises.notes:
Smith Machine Deficit Row Lengthened Partials
(Extend Set) ~3-4 min Pendlay Deficit Row Helms Row Focus on getting a big stretch and touch your stomach/chest on each rep! Once you hit the Last Set RPE on the final set, switch to partial reps. These should be the bottom half of the ROM. Stop once you have 0-1 half reps left in the tank.

exercises.name: Push #2
exercises.notes:
High-Cable Cuffed Lateral Raise Lengthened Partials
(Extend Set) ~1-2 min High-Cable Lateral Raise DB Lateral Raise Focus on squeezing your lateral delt to move the weight. Once you hit failure on the final set, continue with lengthened partials in the bottom half of the ROM, until you can no longer achieve a full half rep.

exercises.name: Legs #2
exercises.notes: Barbell RDL N/A ~3-5 min DB RDL Deadlift The RPE is intentionally low here because these will cause a lot of muscle damage. Don't be tempted to go too heavy. To keep tension on the hamstrings, stop about 75% of the way to full lockout on each rep (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the range of motion).

Weak Points #2
exercises.notes: Weak Point Exercise 1 (optional) N/A ~1-3 min Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.

exercises.name: Pull #1

exercises.name: Push #2

(Extend Set) ~2-3 min Wide-Grip Pull-Up Wide-Grip Machine Pulldown Think about pulling your elbows "down" and "in". Once you hit the Last Set RPE on the final set, switch to partial reps. These should be the top half of the ROM. Stop once you have 0-1 half reps left in the tank.

exercises.name: Push #1
exercises.notes: Meadows Incline DB Lateral Raise Failure ~1-2 min Cuffed Behind-The-Back Lateral Raise DB Lateral Raise Lie down on your side on a ~30° bench. Start with the DB in front of you, raise the DB to parallel to the floor and lower the DB behind you, feeling a deep stretch in your delts, then reverse this motion (2 reps have now been completed). No pausing between reps.

exercises.name: Legs #1
exercises.notes:
Seated Leg Curl Lengthened Partials 
(Extend Set) ~1-2 min Lying Leg Curl Nordic Ham Curl Lean forward over the machine to get a maximum stretch in your hamstrings. Once you hit failure on the final set, continue with lengthened partials in the top half of the ROM, until you can no longer achieve a full half rep.

exercises.name: Arms & Weak Points #1
exercises.notes: Weak Point Exercise 1 (optional) N/A ~1-3 min Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.

exercises.name: Pull #2
exercises.notes:
Pendlay Deficit Row Lengthened Partials
(Extend Set) ~3-4 min Smith Machine Deficit Row DB Row Stand on a bumper plate. Focus on getting a big stretch and touch your stomach/chest on each rep! Once you hit the Last Set RPE on the final set, switch to partial reps. These should be the bottom half of the ROM. Stop once you have 0-1 half reps left in the tank.

exercises.name: Push #2
exercises.notes:
High-Cable Cuffed Lateral Raise Lengthened Partials
(Extend Set) ~1-2 min High-Cable Lateral Raise DB Lateral Raise Focus on squeezing your lateral delt to move the weight. Once you hit failure on the final set, continue with lengthened partials in the bottom half of the ROM, until you can no longer achieve a full half rep.

exercises.name: Legs #2
exercises.notes: DB RDL N/A ~3-5 min Barbell RDL Deadlift The RPE is intentionally low here because these will cause a lot of muscle damage. Don't be tempted to go too heavy. To keep tension on the hamstrings, stop about 75% of the way to full lockout on each rep (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the range of motion).

exercises.name: Arms & Weak Points #2
exercises.notes: Weak Point Exercise 1 (optional) N/A ~1-3 min Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.
